title: Search Indexing Pipeline
triggers: index product catalog changes for search
transactional data updates should reach a search index
background worker updates the search index

requiredNodes.label: Application Service
requiredNodes.description: Transactional service that owns the source data.

requiredNodes.label: Source Datastore
requiredNodes.description: System of record where source entities are written.

requiredNodes.label: Indexing Worker
requiredNodes.description: Background process that transforms source data into searchable documents.

requiredNodes.label: Search Index
requiredNodes.description: Derived store optimized for search queries.

optionalNodes.label: Change Queue
optionalNodes.description: Optional buffer for asynchronous indexing requests.

optionalNodes.label: Search API
optionalNodes.description: Separate read path for serving search requests from the index.

invariants.rule: The Source Datastore should remain the system of record, not the Search Index.
invariants.rationale: Search indices are derived projections optimized for query patterns.

invariants.rule: Changes from the source system must flow to the Search Index through an indexing step.
invariants.rationale: A worker or equivalent transformation stage defines the indexing pipeline.

invariants.rule: The indexing path should be modeled as asynchronous unless the prompt explicitly requires inline updates.
invariants.rationale: Search indexing is commonly decoupled from transactional writes.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt is about hot read caching with direct database fallback.
antiPatterns.rationale: That is a cache-aside pattern, not a derived search projection.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt centers on user-facing retrieval for LLM answers rather than indexing source records.
antiPatterns.rationale: That fits RAG retrieval more than search indexing infrastructure.

clarifications.question: Should indexing be triggered directly from the source datastore or through a queue?
clarifications.reason: Both topologies are common, and the queue adds an explicit buffering component.

clarifications.question: Do you want the search read path shown separately from the write-side indexing flow?
clarifications.reason: Some diagrams include only index maintenance, while others also show query serving.
